title: glechoma lagopsis
intro: Glechoma lagopsis, also known as Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Glechoma lagopsis include relief from colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and improve sleep quality. In herbal preparations, Glechoma lagopsis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
